_comment: RATCHET FLOOR for the CORPUS-COVERS-MATRIX exhaustiveness check in conformance/ci/gate.mjs. Like the mutation break:87 floor (conformance/mutation/stryker.conf.json) and the .NET parity MIN_TEST_COUNT: the gate FAILS only on REGRESSION below these numbers — coverage may freely rise; it may never silently fall. Recompute the live numbers with `node conformance/ci/gate.mjs --print-coverage` and, when they legitimately improve, raise the floor here in the same commit (never lower it to make a red gate green).

scenarioMappableAngleCoverage._meaning: The exhaustiveness invariant: EVERY discovery behavior on a scenario-producing angle (D5 fixture-derived + D7 negative-paths) MUST be represented by a corpus scenario's behaviorIds. These two angles are the ones whose behavior-ids are authored to map 1:1 onto runnable scenarios; the gate enforces 100% and prints any uncovered behavior on failure.

d11SurfaceCoverage._meaning: Headline breadth metric: the fraction of the WHOLE D11 surface (every distinct behavior-id across all 10 discovery angles, including the ones authored under schema/spec/mutation naming that do NOT map 1:1 onto a scenario id) that a corpus behaviorId touches. Reported for transparency and ratcheted as a floor; it is NOT expected to be 100% (the un-touched remainder is the enumerated Part-2 authoring backlog the reconciliation already names).
